title: Strategic Action Catalog for Qualitative Peers

actions.description: Acquired AirTrunk, Asia-Pacific's largest data center platform, for an enterprise value exceeding A$24 billion. AirTrunk operates hyperscale data centers across Australia, Japan, Singapore, Malaysia, and Hong Kong.
actions.stated_rationale: Blackstone positioned itself as the world's largest investor in data centers, capitalizing on AI-driven demand for digital infrastructure. CEO Schwarzman described data centers as a core conviction theme.
actions.metric_impact: Added significant digital infrastructure AUM to the Real Estate and Infrastructure segments. AirTrunk platform spans five APAC markets.

actions.description: Acquired majority ownership position in Jersey Mike's Subs, a leading U.S. quick-service restaurant franchisor with 3,000+ locations.
actions.stated_rationale: Continue to invest in high-growth franchise businesses with proven operating models and strong unit economics.
actions.metric_impact: Significant PE portfolio addition. Transaction completed January 2025.

actions.description: Partnered with Digital Realty on a $7 billion hyperscale data center development joint venture to build new data center capacity in major U.S. markets.
actions.stated_rationale: AI-driven explosion in data center demand creates multi-decade investment opportunity. Partnership structure allows capital-efficient scaling.
actions.metric_impact: $7 billion joint venture commitment; expands U.S. data center portfolio alongside AirTrunk APAC platform.

actions.description: Scaled private wealth distribution platform to $260 billion in AUM, multiples the size of the next-largest competitor. Expanded wirehouses, RIA platforms, and global wealth channel partnerships.
actions.stated_rationale: Private wealth is a primary growth lever; individual investors are massively underallocated to alternatives. Perpetual capital vehicles (BREIT, BCRED, BXPE) serve as anchor products.
actions.metric_impact: $260B private wealth AUM; perpetual capital strategies now comprise 46% of fee-earning AUM.

actions.description: Grew Credit & Insurance segment AUM by 21% year-over-year to $388.7 billion, with over $100 billion in inflows during 2024. Expanded direct lending, private credit, and insurance-linked strategies.
actions.stated_rationale: Credit and insurance represents the largest secular growth opportunity in alternatives. Banks continue to retreat from lending, creating structural demand for private credit.
actions.metric_impact: Credit & Insurance AUM grew from ~$320B to $388.7B (21% YoY); $100B+ inflows in 2024; $30.3B Q1 2025 inflows.

actions.description: Completed acquisition of the remaining 37% of Global Atlantic Financial Group for approximately $2.7 billion in cash, bringing KKR's ownership to 100%. Global Atlantic is a leading fixed annuity and life insurance provider with $158 billion in assets.
actions.stated_rationale: Full ownership enables complete integration of insurance and asset management platforms, creating a scaled permanent capital engine. Global Atlantic provides proprietary capital for KKR's investment strategies.
actions.metric_impact: Added $268B perpetual capital (42% of total AUM). Global Atlantic serves 2M+ policyholders. Incremental FRE from insurance margin.

actions.description: Increased investment in KKR Strategic Holdings by $1.1 billion, scaling permanent balance sheet capital dedicated to long-term operating company investments. Projected operating earnings targets: $350M by 2026, $700M by 2028, $1.1B by 2030.
actions.stated_rationale: Strategic Holdings provides a differentiated source of recurring operating earnings alongside traditional asset management fees and carried interest, diversifying KKR's revenue mix.
actions.metric_impact: Targets $1.1B operating earnings by 2030; $1.1B additional capital deployed.
actions.timeline: Announced at 2024 Investor Day; ongoing capital deployment through 2025

actions.description: Expanded Asia-Pacific investment through country-led franchise model in Japan, India, and Southeast Asia. Ramped platform deals in India's financial services and renewables sectors. Led the EUR 22 billion Telecom Italia NetCo carve-out in Europe.
actions.stated_rationale: Asia-Pacific represents the fastest-growing private markets region. Country-led franchise approach provides local sourcing and execution capabilities. India's financial services and renewables offer structural growth.
actions.metric_impact: EUR 22B Telecom Italia NetCo deal (largest European infrastructure transaction); expanded India financial services and renewables portfolio.

actions.description: Launched K-Series semi-liquid private markets products for the wealth management channel, spanning private equity (K-PE) and infrastructure (K-Infra), targeting HNW and UHNW investors through wirehouse and RIA partnerships.
actions.stated_rationale: Individual investors remain significantly underallocated to alternatives. Semi-liquid vehicles address liquidity preference while providing private markets access. Wealth channel is a multi-decade growth opportunity.
actions.metric_impact: Early-stage fundraising; complements Global Atlantic insurance channel as second pillar of non-institutional capital.

actions.description: Acquired Bridge Investment Group, a real estate-focused alternative asset manager, for approximately $1.5 billion, adding real estate capabilities across multifamily, workforce housing, logistics, and renewable energy.
actions.stated_rationale: Expands Apollo's real estate origination platform and adds complementary capabilities in workforce housing and logistics sectors where origination volume is high.
actions.metric_impact: Added approximately $50B in AUM and diversified origination platforms. Completed February 2025.

actions.description: Acquired Argo Infrastructure Partners for approximately $6 billion, expanding infrastructure and clean energy origination capabilities.
actions.stated_rationale: Infrastructure and clean energy represent massive origination opportunities aligned with Apollo's strategy to be a key capital provider for the energy transition and digital infrastructure buildout.
actions.metric_impact: Expanded infrastructure origination platform. Completed January 2025.

actions.description: Achieved record origination volumes exceeding $300 billion in 2025, driven by expansion of 16+ distinct origination platforms including ATLAS (largest platform). Set new five-year target of $275 billion annual origination.
actions.stated_rationale: In an industry where origination capacity is the key to success, Apollo's diversified origination platform provides a structural competitive advantage. Scale origination feeds Athene's permanent capital needs.
actions.metric_impact: $300B+ origination in 2025; 25% YoY AUM growth to $938B; $228B record inflows.
actions.timeline: 2024 Investor Day target set October 2024; results reported through 2025

actions.description: Extended Mubadala multi-billion-dollar strategic partnership focused on global origination opportunities, and launched defined contribution market initiative via ARS acquisition (with Athene and Motive Ventures) to bring guaranteed income products to 401(k) plans.
actions.stated_rationale: Sovereign wealth partnerships provide scaled capital deployment opportunities. Defined contribution market represents a massive untapped channel for retirement services and guaranteed income products.
actions.metric_impact: Multi-billion-dollar partnership extension with Mubadala; new DC market distribution channel via ARS platform.
actions.timeline: Mubadala partnership extended November 2024; ARS investment March 2025

actions.description: Completed acquisition of GCP International (GLP Capital Partners' international business) for $3.7 billion upfront (cash and Ares shares) plus up to $1.5 billion in earn-outs. Transaction adds approximately $42 billion AUM across Japan, Europe, U.S., Brazil, and Vietnam in logistics, digital infrastructure, and renewable energy.
actions.stated_rationale: Establishes Ares as a leading global real assets platform with significant Asia-Pacific exposure, particularly in Japan logistics. Adds high-conviction sectors including digital infrastructure and self-storage.
actions.metric_impact: Added $42B AUM; total real assets AUM exceeded $115B; total firm AUM surpassed $525B. Significant Japan logistics platform.

actions.description: Closed third U.S. senior direct lending fund (SDL III) at $33.6 billion in total available capital, making it one of the largest direct lending funds ever raised. Also achieved final close for sixth European direct lending fund (ACE VI).
actions.stated_rationale: Direct lending is Ares' core franchise and the secular shift from bank lending to private credit continues to accelerate. Scale provides competitive advantage in sourcing and pricing.
actions.metric_impact: $33.6B SDL III total capital; ACE VI final close for European lending; nearly 40% of Q3 2024 capital raised allocated to direct lending.
actions.timeline: SDL III closed during 2024; ACE VI final close 2024

actions.description: Expanded Ares Wealth Management Solutions (AWMS) distribution platform and scaled private investment-grade origination for insurance clients. Grew insurance channel AUM significantly.
actions.stated_rationale: Private investment-grade market is a significant opportunity to scale insurance industry relationships. Wealth management channel represents a multi-year growth lever as individual investors increase alternatives allocation.
actions.metric_impact: Total AUM reached $464B by year-end 2024; insurance and wealth channels growing as share of total inflows.

actions.description: Targeted and achieved mid-$80B+ annual fundraising pace, gathering $64B by September 2024. Expanded product suite across credit, real assets, secondaries, and insurance solutions. Launched private investment-grade strategies targeting insurance capital.
actions.stated_rationale: Broadening the product suite captures secular demand across multiple private markets channels while deepening relationships with existing LPs.
actions.metric_impact: Annual fundraising exceeded mid-$80B target; record year for capital formation.

actions.description: Acquired majority ownership stake in Angel Oak Companies, a leading asset manager delivering mortgage and consumer credit products, expanding Brookfield's $332 billion credit platform with residential mortgage credit capabilities.
actions.stated_rationale: Expands credit platform into residential mortgage credit, a large and growing asset class. Partnership model allows alignment while maintaining Angel Oak's origination expertise.
actions.metric_impact: Added residential mortgage credit capabilities to $332B credit platform. Transaction completed October 2025.

actions.description: Acquired 51% stake in Castlelake's fee-related earnings for approximately $1.5 billion, adding asset-backed credit and aviation finance capabilities. Also announced acquisition of remaining ~26% of Oaktree Capital Management not already owned.
actions.stated_rationale: Strategic credit partnership model builds a diversified, scaled credit platform through aligned partnerships rather than full acquisitions. Oaktree consolidation provides full integration and operational efficiency.
actions.metric_impact: Castlelake adds asset-backed credit expertise; Oaktree full ownership simplifies corporate structure. Combined credit partners now include Oaktree, Castlelake, LCM, 17Capital, Primary Wave, Angel Oak.
actions.timeline: Castlelake announced May 2024; Oaktree full acquisition announced June 2025

actions.description: Raised over $110 billion in capital during 2025, including $3.5 billion for the second vintage of the global transition flagship fund strategy. Fee-bearing capital reached $603 billion with 87% in long-dated or perpetual vehicles.
actions.stated_rationale: Long-dated and perpetual capital provides durable, compounding fee base. Transition fund capitalizes on global decarbonization and energy security trends.
actions.metric_impact: $110B+ raised in 2025; fee-bearing capital of $603B; $134B uncalled commitments; FRE of $3.0B.

actions.description: Expanded AI/digital infrastructure investment strategy, leveraging operational expertise across data center developments. Positioned at intersection of energy transition and AI infrastructure buildout.
actions.stated_rationale: AI infrastructure requires both significant capital and operational expertise in power procurement and data center management. Brookfield's renewable power portfolio provides competitive advantage in securing clean energy for data centers.
actions.metric_impact: Strategic positioning across AI infrastructure and energy transition themes. Growing data center and digital infrastructure portfolio.

actions.description: Completed acquisition of Atalaya Capital Management, an alternative credit manager with over $10 billion AUM, adding asset-based lending and specialty finance capabilities to Blue Owl's credit platform.
actions.stated_rationale: Atalaya's alternative credit capabilities (asset-based lending, consumer finance, real estate credit) complement Blue Owl's existing direct lending franchise and expand the credit platform into new market segments.
actions.metric_impact: Added $10B+ AUM; expanded credit platform into alternative credit and specialty finance.

actions.description: Completed acquisition of Kuvare Asset Management for $750 million plus $250 million preferred equity investment in Kuvare UK Holdings. Added up to $20 billion in insurance-linked AUM and established an insurance solutions platform.
actions.stated_rationale: Insurance represents a growing source of permanent capital for alternative asset managers. Kuvare provides direct insurance asset management capabilities and long-term alignment through preferred equity co-investment.
actions.metric_impact: Added up to $20B AUM; established insurance solutions as a new business line; $1B total investment (acquisition + preferred equity).

actions.description: Completed acquisition of IPI Partners, a digital infrastructure fund manager with approximately $10.5 billion AUM, for approximately $1.0 billion. Partnership with ICONIQ for future digital infrastructure growth.
actions.stated_rationale: Digital infrastructure is a multi-decade growth theme driven by AI and cloud computing. IPI adds scaled data center and digital real estate investment capabilities.
actions.metric_impact: Added $10.5B AUM; established digital infrastructure as a growth strategy within Real Assets platform.

actions.description: Raised GP Stakes VI targeting $13 billion, building on dominant market position with 62% of total GP stakes market ($39.4B total capital raised). Expanded GP Strategic Capital partnership network to 45+ leading alternative asset managers.
actions.stated_rationale: GP stakes provide permanent capital exposure to the growth of alternative asset management industry. Consolidation in alternatives creates increasing demand for GP capital solutions.
actions.metric_impact: 62% market share in GP stakes; 45+ GP partnerships; GP Stakes VI raised $3.5B+ in early fundraising.
actions.timeline: GP Stakes VI launched 2025; ongoing fundraising

actions.description: Completed acquisition of Angelo Gordon, a diversified credit and real estate investing platform, for approximately $2.7 billion in cash and equity. Angelo Gordon now operates as TPG Angelo Gordon with $74 billion in AUM.
actions.stated_rationale: Fundamentally transforms TPG's business mix by adding scaled credit and real estate capabilities. Creates a diversified platform with credit ($85.6B) as the largest single component of AUM. Adds capabilities relevant to insurance clients (direct lending, CLOs, asset-backed credit).
actions.metric_impact: Added $74B AUM; credit became largest segment at $85.6B; AUM increased 65% in first reporting quarter post-close. Total AUM exceeded $230B.

actions.description: Raised $5.8 billion for Rise Climate II fund, the second vintage of TPG's climate-focused impact investing strategy spanning private equity, transition infrastructure, and Global South initiatives.
actions.stated_rationale: Climate investment represents an expanding opportunity set with structural tailwinds from energy transition. Rise Climate provides differentiated impact positioning that attracts ESG-focused capital.
actions.metric_impact: $5.8B Rise Climate II fundraise; expanded multi-strategy climate platform across PE, infrastructure, and Global South.

actions.description: Launched private wealth distribution channel with initial evergreen products, seeding a portfolio on balance sheet before accepting inflows. Raised approximately $1.5 billion within initial months with limited distribution footprint. Planning additional products including multi-strategy private credit interval fund and non-traded REIT.
actions.stated_rationale: Private wealth is a significant untapped growth channel. Insurance clients and wealth channel represent the next phase of TPG's capital diversification beyond institutional LPs.
actions.metric_impact: $1.5B raised in initial months; wealth channel products planned including credit interval fund and non-traded REIT.

actions.description: Targeted and achieved $50B+ annual fundraising in 2025, declaring it a 'breakout year' with multiple flagship campaigns in market (TPG Capital, Healthcare Partners). AUM growth positioned TPG as a scaled multi-strategy platform.
actions.stated_rationale: 2025 represents the first full year demonstrating the integrated platform's fundraising power post-Angelo Gordon. Multiple simultaneous flagship campaigns create diversified inflow streams.
actions.metric_impact: $50B+ annual fundraising target achieved; multiple flagship campaigns in market simultaneously.

actions.description: Under CEO Harvey Schwartz (joined February 2023), implemented comprehensive strategic turnaround: created strategic growth plan tied to client needs, reshaped operating model with expense actions, eliminated underperforming lines, and made leadership/organizational design changes. Set 2028 financial targets: $200B new inflows, $1.9B FRE, $2.8B management fees, 50%+ margins.
actions.stated_rationale: Schwartz's three guiding principles: align growth plan with client needs, reshape operating model for efficiency, and upgrade leadership. 2024 targets were met ahead of schedule, validating the turnaround approach.
actions.metric_impact: 2025 record year: $54B inflows (vs. $40B target), record $477B AUM. 2028 targets: $1.9B FRE, $200B fundraising, 50%+ margins, $6+ DE/share.
actions.timeline: Turnaround initiated February 2023; 2024 targets met; 2028 targets set at 2026 Shareholder Update

actions.description: Launched Fortitude Carlyle Asia Reinsurance (FCA Re), a Bermuda-domiciled Class E reinsurer with $700M+ deployable capital, to accelerate Fortitude Re's growth in the Asian life and annuity market. Equity commitments from Fortitude Re, Carlyle, T&D Insurance, AllianceBernstein, Shinhan Life, and National Pension Service of Korea.
actions.stated_rationale: Asian life and annuity market represents a significant growth opportunity for reinsurance. Multi-partner structure with Asian institutional investors provides local relationships and alignment.
actions.metric_impact: $700M+ deployable capital; new Asian reinsurance sidecar with prestigious co-investors.

actions.description: Expanded wealth distribution, integrated credit and insurance solutions, and accelerated AI adoption across investment and operating functions. Fortitude Re advisory agreement expected to increase Global Credit Fee-earning AUM by $50B.
actions.stated_rationale: Wealth distribution, credit/insurance integration, and AI represent the three highest-growth opportunity areas. Fortitude Re advisory provides scaled insurance asset management revenue.
actions.metric_impact: Fortitude Re advisory adds $50B FAUM and $50M incremental FRE. Wealth and insurance channels growing as share of inflows.
actions.timeline: Ongoing 2024-2025; Fortitude Re advisory effective 2022, scaling through 2025

actions.description: Acquired Perficient, a digital consultancy firm, for $3 billion in an all-cash deal. Also acquired OX2, a Stockholm-based renewable energy development company, and Indostar Home Finance (India affordable housing) for $210 million.
actions.stated_rationale: Perficient enhances digital transformation capabilities for portfolio companies. OX2 positions EQT in European renewable energy development. Indostar extends India presence in affordable housing finance.
actions.metric_impact: $3B Perficient acquisition adds digital consultancy capabilities; OX2 provides renewable energy development pipeline; Indostar enters Indian affordable housing.
actions.timeline: Perficient completed October 2024; OX2 and Indostar completed 2024

actions.description: Closed EQT Infrastructure VI at EUR 21.5 billion, exceeding its EUR 20 billion target and hitting hard cap. This made it one of the largest infrastructure funds globally and the largest EQT infrastructure fund to date.
actions.stated_rationale: Infrastructure is a core EQT strategy with strong secular tailwinds (digitalization, decarbonization, essential services). Fund size reflects LP confidence and track record.
actions.metric_impact: EUR 21.5B fund close; exceeded target by EUR 1.5B. One of the largest infrastructure funds globally.
actions.timeline: Fundraising through 2024; closed at hard cap

actions.description: Launched BPEA Private Equity Fund IX targeting $12.5 billion (hard cap $14.5 billion) for Asian buyouts. Activated March 2025 with first close receiving $11.4 billion in commitments. By January 2026, received approximately $14 billion in commitments approaching hard cap.
actions.stated_rationale: Asia represents the fastest-growing PE market globally. BPEA integration (completed 2022) has created a scaled Asian platform capable of deploying $12.5B+ in a single fund vintage.
actions.metric_impact: $14B commitments approaching $14.5B hard cap; demonstrates successful BPEA integration and scaled Asian fundraising.
actions.timeline: Activated March 2025; first close April 2025; approaching hard cap January 2026

actions.description: Built out private wealth capabilities including EQT Nexus platform, adding senior team hires, distribution bank partnerships, and new product preparations. Launched EQT Nexus Infrastructure targeting European and Asian wealth channel clients.
actions.stated_rationale: Private wealth channel represents significant growth opportunity for European alternative managers. EQT Nexus distribution partnerships with European banks provide scalable distribution infrastructure.
actions.metric_impact: EQT Nexus fundraising accelerated; infrastructure variant launched; distribution partnerships expanded across Europe and Asia.

actions.description: Completed acquisition of Empira Group, a premier European real estate investment platform, to enhance real estate capabilities. Partners Group expects $4 billion of platform growth from the acquisition.
actions.stated_rationale: Real estate is entering a new paradigm for investing, and Empira's capabilities enhance Partners Group's position as a global direct real estate investor. Expected to drive $4B in platform growth.
actions.metric_impact: Expected $4B platform growth; enhanced European real estate direct investing capabilities.

actions.description: Established strategic partnership with BlackRock to develop first-of-its-kind model portfolio providing individual U.S. investors access to private markets. Also formed distribution partnerships with Deutsche Bank, PGIM, Generali Investments, Lincoln Financial, Erste Asset Management, BBVA Asset Management, and Perpetual Group.
actions.stated_rationale: Solving the allocation gap in private wealth requires scalable distribution partnerships with major financial institutions. BlackRock model portfolio is a first-of-its-kind solution for retail investor access.
actions.metric_impact: Multiple tier-one distribution partnerships signed; BlackRock model portfolio expected to launch H1 2025.
actions.timeline: BlackRock partnership announced September/December 2024; additional partnerships throughout 2024-2025

actions.description: Launched private markets royalties as a fifth asset class (May 2024) and extended evergreen fund platform with seven new offerings. Evergreen funds account for 30% of global AUM with the U.S. PE evergreen fund reaching $15.5 billion (largest in market).
actions.stated_rationale: Royalties provide a new source of uncorrelated returns from intellectual property cash flows. Evergreen fund expansion addresses structural demand for perpetual private markets vehicles from wealth and institutional clients.
actions.metric_impact: 30% of AUM in evergreen products; U.S. PE evergreen at $15.5B (largest globally); royalties launched as 5th asset class; seven new evergreen offerings.
actions.timeline: Royalties launched May 2024; evergreen expansion ongoing 2024-2025

actions.description: Achieved double-digit AUM growth in 2025, reaching approximately USD 170-185 billion in AUM. Raised over $110 billion during the period. Set long-term target of $450 billion AUM by 2033.
actions.stated_rationale: Structural AUM and inflow growth driven by strong investor demand, U.S. evergreen expansion, and distribution partnership proliferation compounds fee revenues and margins.
actions.metric_impact: AUM reached ~$185B by year-end 2025; $450B AUM target by 2033.

actions.description: Scaled StepStone Private Wealth Solutions (SPWS) from $5 billion to $12.1 billion AUM in approximately 15 months, tripling assets. Lowered investment minimums and eliminated accredited investor requirements for several wealth products to broaden access.
actions.stated_rationale: Democratizing private markets access is a core strategic priority. Rapid SPWS growth validates demand from financial advisors and their clients for accessible private markets solutions.
actions.metric_impact: SPWS AUM tripled to $12.1B (as of September 2025); investment minimums lowered; accredited investor requirements eliminated for select products.

actions.description: Partnered with Utmost to provide UK savers access to private markets (PE, venture, credit, infrastructure) via unit-linked products. Expanded international distribution for private markets solutions.
actions.stated_rationale: UK pension and savings market represents significant untapped demand for private markets exposure. Utmost partnership provides regulatory-compliant distribution infrastructure.
actions.metric_impact: New UK distribution channel via Utmost unit-linked products; broadened geographic reach for SPWS platform.

actions.description: Launched StepStone Academy, an education platform for financial professionals offering on-demand continuing education credits and private markets training. Expanded evergreen product offerings across PE, credit, infrastructure, and venture capital.
actions.stated_rationale: Education-driven distribution strategy deepens advisor relationships and builds long-term commitment to private markets allocation. Advisors who understand private markets better are more likely to allocate.
actions.metric_impact: StepStone Academy launched; expanded advisor engagement and education pipeline. Total capital responsibility reached $723B including $199B AUM.

actions.description: Expanded evergreen fund platform to $12B+ AUM across 10 funds with launches of: Private Secondary Fund (HLPSF, March 2025), Venture Capital and Growth Fund (HLVCG, May 2025), and Infrastructure Fund (HLPIF) -- the first private infrastructure offering available to U.S. retail investors via Republic digital platform.
actions.stated_rationale: Evergreen product proliferation addresses structural demand for accessible private markets vehicles. First-mover in retail infrastructure demonstrates commitment to democratizing private markets.
actions.metric_impact: Evergreen platform exceeded $12B AUM; 10 evergreen funds; first retail infrastructure product in U.S.
actions.timeline: HLPSF March 2025; HLVCG May 2025; HLPIF launched 2024-2025

actions.description: Expanded Cobalt LP proprietary data and analytics platform through strategic collaboration with Northern Trust Asset Servicing. Invested in Daphne Technologies fundraising distribution platform. Cobalt database covers 58,600+ funds across all private markets.
actions.stated_rationale: Technology and data are competitive moats in private markets. Cobalt platform licensing and partnerships generate revenue while deepening institutional relationships. Daphne investment enhances fundraising process efficiency.
actions.metric_impact: Cobalt covers 58,600+ funds; Northern Trust distribution channel; Daphne Technologies integration.
actions.timeline: Northern Trust collaboration announced October 2024; Daphne investment 2024

actions.description: Grew total assets under management and supervision to $940B+ ($130B discretionary, $810B+ non-discretionary) as of June 2024. Expanded digital distribution through Republic platform and lowered minimums across evergreen products.
actions.stated_rationale: Broader distribution through digital platforms and lower minimums captures growing retail demand for private markets exposure. Advisory mandates ($810B+) provide stable revenue base while discretionary AUM drives higher-margin growth.
actions.metric_impact: $940B+ total capital; $130B discretionary; digital distribution via Republic; expanded access.

actions.description: Acquired American Equity Life Insurance Company (AEL) to create Brookfield Wealth Solutions, nearly doubling wealth solutions earnings compared to prior year. Established scaled insurance and annuity platform.
actions.stated_rationale: Insurance provides permanent capital and liability-driven investment opportunities aligned with Brookfield's long-duration real asset portfolios. AEL acquisition creates an at-scale platform for compounding over multiple decades.
actions.metric_impact: Wealth solutions earnings nearly doubled YoY; established scaled insurance platform. Brookfield Wealth Solutions now manages significant annuity portfolio.

actions.description: Expanded Brookfield Wealth Solutions insurance operations internationally, scaling pension risk transfer capabilities and growing presence in the UK and Japan markets alongside the core North American annuity business.
actions.stated_rationale: UK and Japan are the two largest pension risk transfer and annuity markets outside North America. Geographic diversification of insurance liabilities reduces concentration risk and expands addressable market.
actions.metric_impact: New UK and Japan insurance presence; diversified beyond North American annuity market.

actions.description: Completed corporate restructuring including transfer of asset management operations to publicly listed BAM. Ended year with record $160 billion of available capital for deployment, including $68 billion of cash, financial assets, and undrawn credit lines. Set target of 25% annual DE/share growth through 2030.
actions.stated_rationale: Corporate simplification clarifies the value proposition: BN as diversified holding company, BAM as pure-play asset manager. Record available capital positions the firm for both market opportunities and strategic acquisitions.
actions.metric_impact: $160B available capital; DE/share target of $10.40 by 2030 (25% annual growth); corporate structure simplified.
actions.timeline: BAM restructuring completed 2024; financial targets set 2025

actions.description: Raised EUR 15.2 billion ($16.8B) for Senior Debt Partners V, making it Europe's largest direct lending fund ever raised. Also closed Strategic Equity Fund V at $11 billion and Europe Mid-Market Fund II at EUR 3.0 billion.
actions.stated_rationale: Record fund sizes demonstrate LP confidence in ICG's track record and the firm's ability to deploy capital effectively in European credit markets. SDP V's record size reinforces ICG's leadership in European direct lending.
actions.metric_impact: SDP V: EUR 15.2B (Europe's largest direct lending fund); SEF V: $11B; EMMF II: EUR 3.0B. Total $24B raised in FY2025.

actions.description: Opened new offices in Copenhagen (2023), Toronto (2024), Zurich, and Munich (2025) as part of 'scaling out' strategy. Expanded geographic footprint to access new LP markets and investment opportunities in the DACH region and North America.
actions.stated_rationale: 'Scaling out' strategy targets new geographies to diversify LP base, access local deal flow, and expand distribution capabilities. DACH region and Canada represent significant institutional capital pools.
actions.metric_impact: Four new offices opened in 18 months; expanded geographic coverage for both fundraising and investing.

actions.description: Formed strategic distribution partnership with Amundi to broaden access to ICG's private markets strategies. Total AUM grew to $112 billion (14% YoY) with fee-earning AUM of $75 billion (8% growth). Targeting at least $55 billion in fundraising over next four years.
actions.stated_rationale: Amundi partnership provides access to Amundi's broad institutional and retail distribution network, enabling ICG to reach investor segments beyond traditional institutional LPs.
actions.metric_impact: AUM reached $112B (14% YoY growth); FAUM $75B; $55B+ fundraising target over four years.
actions.timeline: Amundi partnership announced 2024; fundraising targets set FY2025

actions.description: Completed going-private acquisition by GTCR, a leading PE firm, for $35.25 per share in cash (~$2.7 billion equity value). Transaction launched a new era of strategic growth under PE ownership.
actions.stated_rationale: GTCR provides PE capital, operational expertise, and M&A capabilities to accelerate AssetMark's growth. GTCR has substantial investment expertise in financial technology and wealth management sectors.
actions.metric_impact: $2.7B equity valuation; transitioned from public to private ownership; new strategic growth mandate.

actions.description: Announced strategic alliance with Morningstar Wealth and acquisition of approximately $12 billion in assets from Morningstar's Turnkey Asset Management Platform (TAMP) business. Financial advisors on Morningstar TAMP will transition to AssetMark's platform.
actions.stated_rationale: Consolidation of TAMP market through acquisition of complementary platform assets. Morningstar TAMP advisors gain access to AssetMark's industry-leading service, technology, and curated investment strategies.
actions.metric_impact: Added approximately $12B platform assets; expanded advisor base; TAMP consolidation play.

actions.description: Continued platform enhancement under GTCR ownership, expanding technology capabilities, investment product offerings, and business consulting services for 9,200+ financial advisor relationships serving 261,000+ investor households. Platform assets exceeded $119 billion.
actions.stated_rationale: Technology-enabled platform is the core value proposition. Enhanced technology and expanded product capabilities deepen advisor relationships and increase wallet share per advisor.
actions.metric_impact: $119B+ platform assets; 9,200+ advisor relationships; 261,000+ investor households; 1,000+ employees.
